app.name: Copilot Cowork Estimator
app.tagline: Estimate Microsoft Copilot Cowork credits consumption
app.disclaimer: This tool produces indicative estimates. Values do not represent official or contractual Microsoft pricing.

help.title: User Guide
help.subtitle: Everything you need to estimate Copilot Cowork credits.

intro.title: What this app is
intro.body: Copilot Cowork Estimator is a planning tool that estimates the consumption and cost of Copilot Credits for your organization. It runs entirely in the browser: no data is sent to external servers and everything is stored locally. Estimates are based on transparent, editable assumptions — not official Microsoft pricing.

companies.title: Managing companies
companies.body: Companies are the main container for all simulations. Create a company for each client or organization, then add linked estimation scenarios.
companies.createBody: Go to Companies → New Company. Name and number of employees are all you need to start. Industry, country, tags and notes are optional.
companies.archiveBody: Archive a company to exclude it from dashboard totals without deleting it. You can restore it at any time.
companies.deleteBody: Deletion requires confirmation. If the company has linked scenarios, you must type its name to confirm: all scenarios will be removed.
companies.duplicateBody: You can duplicate a company with just the profile data, or with all linked scenarios (new IDs are assigned).

segments.title: Workforce segmentation
segments.body: Segmentation divides the workforce into homogeneous groups to produce more realistic estimates. Each segment has a headcount, enablement percentages and a usage profile.
segments.addBody: Use predefined templates (Basic, Office, Manufacturing, Service) to start quickly, or add segments manually.
segments.calcBody: Every segment change immediately recalculates enabled users, active users, estimated credits and cost. The calculation uses the min/mid/max range.
segments.baselineBody: You can save the segmentation as a company baseline and copy it into new scenarios. Changes in a scenario do not alter the baseline.
segments.excludeBody: Use the 'Include in calculation' toggle to temporarily exclude a segment without deleting it. Useful for simulating partial rollouts.

wizard.title: Guided wizard for new scenarios
wizard.body: The wizard guides scenario creation through 7 progressive steps: you don't need to know all the advanced options to get a valid estimate.
wizard.stepsBody: Scenario (name + company) → Workforce → Usage profiles → Models & assumptions → Funding & budget → Review → Save & share.
wizard.draftBody: The wizard automatically saves a local draft. If you accidentally close the browser, the draft will be offered when you return.
wizard.reviewBody: The review shows min/mid/max credits and costs, per-segment breakdown and all warnings. You can go back to previous steps to fix issues.

profiles.title: Usage profiles
profiles.body: Usage profiles translate workforce segmentation into estimable monthly task volumes. Light, Medium and Heavy are the standard profiles; you can create custom ones.
profiles.standardBody: Light (occasional use), Medium (regular use) and Heavy (intensive use) are always available and cannot be deleted or directly edited.
profiles.customBody: Duplicate a standard profile to create your own variant. You can modify monthly tasks, the default model and advanced factors.
profiles.factorsBody: Factors (context, tools, runtime, browser, image) multiply the base consumption. The standard value is 1. Only modify them if you have specific needs.
profiles.impactBody: Each profile shows estimated credits and cost for a single active user, calculated with the default assumption pack.

taskPresets.title: Task presets & AI models
taskPresets.body: Task presets describe types of AI activities (deep research, document analysis, agent tasks...). Each preset has a min/mid/max credit band and complexity factors.
taskPresets.presetBody: Use system presets as a reference. You can duplicate and customize them. Presets using browser or image generation may have significantly higher consumption.
taskPresets.modelBody: Each model has a 'model factor' that multiplies the base consumption. Auto is the default model. You can enable/disable models and modify the factor (on editable models only).
taskPresets.packsBody: The assumption pack contains the credit bands (min/mid/max) for light/medium/heavy tasks and multiplier factors. Values are indicative estimates, not official pricing.

quickstart.title: Quick start in 4 steps
quickstart.step1Body: Go to Companies and create a new client with its name and number of employees.
quickstart.step2Body: Start a new scenario, pick the company and define the workforce segments (e.g. Sales, IT, Managers).
quickstart.step3Body: For each segment set headcount, enabled and active user percentages, usage profile and preferred AI model.
quickstart.step4Body: The scenario automatically computes monthly/annual credits and cost as a min/mid/max range, with a per-segment breakdown.

concepts.title: Key concepts
concepts.creditsBody: The consumption unit of the Cowork model. Each task consumes credits based on model, context, tools and complexity.
concepts.rangeBody: Estimates are not a single number but a range. The midpoint is the main value; min and max show the best and worst case.
concepts.profileBody: Light, Medium and Heavy describe how intensively a segment uses Copilot each month. You can also create custom ones.
concepts.assumptionBody: A versioned set of calculation rules (credit bands, model factors). Kept separate from company data and updatable.

dataPrivacy.title: Data privacy
dataPrivacy.body: All data stays in your browser's localStorage. Export a JSON backup regularly from Settings to avoid losing your work: clearing browser data also deletes your simulations.

faq.a1: No. They are planning estimates based on assumptions. They do not represent a Microsoft quote or contractual price.
faq.a2: Exclusively in the browser (localStorage). No server, no login, no tracking.
faq.a3: Yes, via JSON export or (coming soon) a shareable link. The recipient can import the configuration.
faq.q4: How do I update calculation assumptions?
faq.a4: In the Assumptions section you can review and duplicate assumption packs to create custom versions.

welcome.title: Welcome to Copilot Cowork Estimator
welcome.body: Let's walk through the app in a few steps. You can skip the tour anytime and restart it from the Help page.

nav.title: Navigation
nav.body: From here you reach Dashboard, Companies, Scenarios, Assumptions and Compare. On mobile use the menu at the top left.

companies.title: Manage companies
companies.body: Start by creating a company with its employee count. Each company can have multiple estimation scenarios.

companyNew.title: New company
companyNew.body: Click here to create a new company. Name and number of employees are all you need to start.

companiesFilters.title: Search and filter
companiesFilters.body: Search companies by name, sort by cost or headcount, and show/hide archived ones.

scenarios.title: Estimation scenarios
scenarios.body: A scenario models the workforce in segments and computes credits and costs as a range.

wizardHeader.title: Guided wizard
wizardHeader.body: The wizard guides you through 7 steps: scenario name, company, workforce, profiles, models, funding and review. You can save a draft at any time.

profiles.title: Usage profiles
profiles.body: Profiles (Light, Medium, Heavy) define how many monthly tasks an active user runs. You can duplicate them and create custom profiles.

profilesList.title: Profile cards
profilesList.body: Each card shows the task distribution and estimated cost per active user. Click 'Details' to see advanced factors.

assumptionsTabs.title: Assumptions section
assumptionsTabs.body: Here you find task presets, AI models and assumption packs. Values are indicative estimates — not official Microsoft pricing.

tasksList.title: Task presets
tasksList.body: Each preset has a min/mid/max credit band and complexity indicators. Presets using browser or image generation may have higher consumption.

modelsList.title: AI models
modelsList.body: Enable or disable models, and modify the model factor for editable models. The factor multiplies the base task consumption.

segmentsAdd.title: Add segments
segmentsAdd.body: Create workforce segments: each segment has headcount, enablement percentages and a usage profile.

segmentsTable.title: Live calculation
segmentsTable.body: The table shows enabled and active users, credits and estimated cost per segment. Values update in real time.

theme.title: Theme and language
theme.body: Switch between light/dark theme and English or Italian at any time.

help.title: Need help?
help.body: The full guide is always here. Look for the info icons next to values for quick explanations.

restartStep.title: Restart the tour anytime
restartStep.body: This button restarts the guided tour at any time, from any page. Once you've seen it, the tour won't start on its own again.

hints.monthlyCredits: Sum of estimated monthly credits across all active scenarios, for the selected range (min/mid/max).
hints.monthlyCost: Estimated monthly cost, obtained by valuing the credits at the funding plan's price per credit.
hints.annualCost: Annual projection of the monthly cost (× 12). It is an estimate, not a spending commitment.
hints.warnings: Number of functional warnings across scenarios: custom assumptions, exceeded budgets, incomplete data, etc.
hints.rangeSelector: Change the displayed value: Min (optimistic), Mid (expected), Max (conservative).
hints.enabledPct: Percentage of people in the segment assigned a Copilot license.
hints.activePct: Percentage of enabled users who actually use Copilot on a recurring basis.
hints.usageProfile: Average usage intensity of the segment: defines how many light/medium/heavy tasks each user runs per month.
hints.preferredModel: AI model used by the segment. More powerful models consume more credits per task.
hints.assumptionPack: Versioned set of calculation rules applied to the scenario. Determines credit bands and factors.
hints.headcount: Total number of people in this workforce segment.
hints.modelFactor: Multiplier reflecting the higher credit consumption of more capable models.
hints.creditBands: Estimated credits per task by intensity (light/medium/heavy), in min/mid/max values.
hints.costPerActive: Average monthly cost per user actively using Copilot.
hints.costPerEnabled: Average monthly cost per user assigned a license.
hints.totalEmployees: Total number of employees in the organization. Used to compare segment coverage.
hints.estimatedKnowledgeWorkers: Estimated number of users whose work is compatible with Copilot (knowledge workers). Useful for sizing coverage.
hints.legalName: Official legal name, if different from the commercial name used in estimates.
hints.region: Geographic area or reference market, useful for segmenting multi-country portfolios.
hints.companyTags: Free labels to classify and filter the company in the portfolio.
hints.companyCurrency: Default currency for costing this company. Overrides the global default.
hints.companyMonthlyCost: Sum of estimated monthly costs (midpoint) across all active scenarios for this company.
hints.companyAnnualCost: Annual projection of the sum of monthly costs (× 12).
hints.pricePerCredit: Unit price per credit in the PAYG plan. Determines the final economic cost.
hints.existingCredits: Credits already available in the tenant. Consumed before PAYG credits.
hints.prepaidCredits: Credits purchased at a discounted price. Consumed after existing credits.
hints.discount: Percentage discount applied to the price per credit in the prepaid plan.
hints.monthlyBudget: Maximum monthly budget available for Copilot. Triggers an alert if the estimated cost exceeds it.

wizard.title: New Scenario
wizard.exitBody: You have unsaved changes. What do you want to do?
wizard.draftRecoveryBody: There is an autosaved draft from {{date}}. Do you want to resume it?
wizard.allSegmentsExcluded: All segments are excluded from the calculation. You can save as a draft or include at least one segment.
wizard.noSegments: Add at least one segment included in the calculation.

scenarioSetup.title: Create your scenario
scenarioSetup.noCompanies: No companies saved. Create the first one now.
scenarioSetup.nameDuplicate: A scenario with this name already exists.

workforce.title: Workforce segmentation
workforce.subtitle: Define the groups of users who will use Copilot.
workforce.useBaselineDesc: Load the segments saved in the company as a starting point.
workforce.useTemplateDesc: Choose a predefined template to start quickly.
workforce.addManualDesc: Create segments one at a time.

usageProfiles.title: Usage profiles
usageProfiles.subtitle: Review and customize the profile for each segment.

modelsAssumptions.title: Models & assumptions
modelsAssumptions.subtitle: Select the AI model and assumption pack for the calculation.

fundingBudget.title: Funding plan & budget
fundingBudget.subtitle: Configure how credits are economically valued.

review.title: Scenario review
review.subtitle: Check results before saving.
review.canSaveReviewed: The scenario is complete and can be saved as Reviewed.
review.mustFixErrors: Fix blocking errors or save as a draft.

saveShare.title: Save & share
saveShare.subtitle: Save your scenario and choose how to share it.
